questions.text: The enzyme found in saliva responsible for the digestion of carbohydrates is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Lysozyme is an enzyme that plays a role in the immune system by acting as an antimicrobial agent. Lysozyme is found in various bodily fluids, including saliva, tears, and mucus. It functions by breaking down the cell walls of certain bacteria, thereby helping to protect the body from infections.

The enzyme found in saliva responsible for the digestion of carbohydrates is amylase. Amylase breaks down complex carbohydrates, such as starches, into simpler sugars like maltose and glucose. Salivary amylase is produced by the salivary glands and is an important component of the initial stages of the digestive process, helping to initiate the breakdown of carbohydrates in the mouth before food travels to the stomach and further along the digestive tract.

Pepsin is an enzyme that plays a crucial role in the digestion of proteins. It is produced in the stomach, where it helps break down large protein molecules into smaller peptides. Pepsin works in an acidic environment, which is provided by the gastric acid in the stomach.

Trypsinogen is also an inactive precursor enzyme, similar to pepsinogen, but it is involved in the digestion of proteins in the small intestine. Trypsinogen is secreted by the pancreas into the small intestine, and it is activated to trypsin, an active enzyme, during the digestive process.

Lipase is an enzyme that plays a crucial role in the digestion of fats (lipids) in the digestive system. Lipases break down complex fats into simpler fatty acids and glycerol, which can then be absorbed by the body.

questions.explanation: The enzyme found in saliva responsible for the digestion of carbohydrates is amylase. Amylase breaks down complex carbohydrates, such as starches, into simpler sugars like maltose and glucose. options.explanation: An antigen is a molecule or molecular structure that the immune system recognizes as foreign or non-self. It can stimulate an immune response, leading to the production of antibodies or activation of immune cells. Antigens can be found on the surface of pathogens, such as bacteria or viruses, as well as on the surface of cells, including those from transplanted organs or cancer cells. The immune system's recognition of antigens is crucial for mounting effective responses against infections and other challenges.

Antitoxins are antibodies that neutralize toxins produced by bacteria, viruses, or other pathogens. When the body encounters a toxin, the immune system may produce specific antitoxins to counteract the harmful effects of the toxin. These antibodies can bind to the toxin, preventing it from causing damage to cells or tissues.

questions.text: The lock and key model of enzyme action was proposed by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Louis Pasteur (1822–1895) was a French chemist and microbiologist who made pioneering contributions to the fields of microbiology and immunology. Louis Pasteur developed the process of pasteurization, which involves heating liquids (such as milk or wine) to a specific temperature to kill or deactivate bacteria and other microorganisms.

Emil Fischer (1890) proposed a Lock and Key model to visualize substrate and enzyme interaction. According to this model, as one specific key can open only a specific lock, in the same manner a specific enzyme can transform only one substrate into products(s). According to Lock and Key Model the active site is a rigid structure. There is no modification or fexibility in the active site before, during or after the enzyme action and it is used only as a template. Later studies did not support this model in all reactions.

Daniel E. Koshland Jr. (1920–2007) was a renowned American biochemist known for his significant contributions to the field of enzymology and molecular biology. Koshland is perhaps best known for proposing the "induced fit" model of enzyme-substrate binding. In this model, the active site of an enzyme undergoes a conformational change upon binding with the substrate, ensuring a more precise fit and enhancing catalytic activity.

The well-known scientific experiment related to the origin of life conducted by Stanley Miller and Harold Urey is often referred to as the "Miller-Urey experiment." Stanley L. Miller and Harold C. Urey conducted this groundbreaking experiment in 1952 to simulate the conditions thought to be present on early Earth. The goal was to investigate whether organic compounds, such as amino acids, the building blocks of proteins, could be formed under these conditions. The experiment involved creating an atmosphere containing gases believed to be present on early Earth (such as methane, ammonia, and water vapor) and subjecting it to simulated lightning (electrical discharge). The result was the production of various organic molecules, including amino acids.

questions.text: Deuterostomes have:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Spiral cleavage is present in protostomes. Spiral cleavage is a type of cell division that occurs in the embryos of some animals. It is characterized by the unequal distribution of the mitotic spindle and cleavage furrows, which results in a spiral pattern of cell divisions. Spiral cleavage is found in most protostomes, including annelids, mollusks, and arthropods. It is not found in deuterostomes, which include chordates, echinoderms, and hemichordates.

No, the mouth of deuterostomes does not develop from the blastopore. Instead, the anus develops from the blastopore in deuterostomes. This is in contrast to protostomes, in which the mouth develops from the blastopore. Deuterostomes are a large group of animals that includes echinoderms, chordates, and hemichordates. The blastopore is the opening that forms in the early embryo from which the gut develops. In deuterostomes, the blastopore becomes the anus, while the mouth develops from a separate opening.

options.text: Mesoderm is formed form developing gut
options.explanation: Deuterostomes have mesoderm formed from the developing gut. Deuterostomes are a group of animals that includes echinoderms, chordates, and hemichordates. They are characterized by the fact that their blastopore develops into the anus, while the mouth develops secondarily. In deuterostomes, the mesoderm is formed by a process called endocoely. Endocoely is a type of embryonic development in which the mesoderm forms from pouches that bud off from the developing gut. The mesoderm is a middle layer of tissue that forms between the ectoderm and endoderm. It gives rise to a variety of tissues and organs, including muscles, bones, blood, and the gonads.The formation of the mesoderm from the developing gut is a unique feature of deuterostomes. It is thought to be an adaptation that allows deuterostomes to grow and develop more complex bodies than their protostome counterparts.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a difference that would allow one to distinguish between a prokaryotic and a eukaryotic cell? Presence of absence of the nucleus Presence or absence of the cell wall Membrane bound versus no membrane bound organelles
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The correct answer is: Presence or absence of the cell wall.Here's why:- Presence or absence of the nucleus: Prokaryotic cells lack a true nucleus, while eukaryotic cells have a membrane-bound nucleus. This is a key distinguishing feature.- Membrane-bound versus no membrane-bound organelles: Eukaryotic cells have membrane-bound organelles like mitochondria, chloroplasts, and a nucleus, while prokaryotic cells do not. This is another key difference.- Presence or absence of the cell wall: Both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells can have cell walls. While it's true that some eukaryotic cells (like animal cells) lack cell walls, many others (like plant cells and fungi) have them. Similarly, some prokaryotic cells (like bacteria) have cell walls, while others (like mycoplasmas) do not. So, the presence or absence of a cell wall is not a reliable way to distinguish between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells.Therefore, the presence or absence of the cell wall is NOT a difference that would allow one to distinguish between a prokaryotic and a eukaryotic cell.

questions.explanation: Eukaryotic cells have nucleus and membrane-bound organelles, which is not the property of prokaryotes.The cell wall is not a distinguishing feature between prokaryotes or eukaryotes.

questions.text: Senescence as an active developmental cellular process in the growth and functioning of a flowering plant, is indicated in :
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Annual plants are a category of plants that complete their entire life cycle, from germination to flowering, seed production, and death, within a single growing season or year. Annuals are known for their relatively short life span compared to biennial or perennial plants.Annual plants have a one-year lifecycle. They germinate from seeds, grow, produce flowers, set seeds, and then die within a single year. Their life cycle is synchronized with the changing seasons.Many annuals are popular choices for garden beds, borders, and containers, providing seasonal bursts of color.Numerous annual plants are cultivated for their edible parts, including vegetables like tomatoes, cucumbers, and lettuce, as well as herbs like basil and cilantro.Annual plants play a significant role in the plant world, providing beauty, sustenance, and ecological importance, particularly in quickly changing or disturbed environments. They are part of the rich tapestry of plant life and contribute to the diversity of ecosystems and landscapes.

Floral parts, also known as the "flower's anatomy," refer to the various structures and components that make up a complete flower. A typical flower consists of several distinct parts, each with its specific function in sexual reproduction. These parts work together to ensure the plant's reproductive success, as they play a role in attracting pollinators, transferring pollen, and producing seeds, which are vital for the plant's future generations.

Vessel elements and tracheid elements are two types of specialized water-conducting cells found in the xylem tissue of vascular plants, responsible for transporting water and minerals from the roots to the rest of the plant. In summary, vessel elements are specialized for efficient water transport and are common in angiosperms. They have perforation plates for unimpeded water flow. Tracheid elements are more primitive in evolutionary terms and are found in gymnosperms. They have bordered pits and play a role in water transport but are less efficient than vessel elements. Both types of cells contribute to the overall function of the xylem tissue in vascular plants.

Leaf abscission, also known simply as "abscission," is the natural process by which plants shed or drop their leaves, typically as a response to seasonal changes or environmental conditions. This process allows plants to conserve water and energy during adverse conditions, such as winter or drought. Leaf abscission is primarily triggered by environmental cues, including changes in temperature, daylight duration, and the plant's internal signaling mechanisms. For deciduous trees and many perennial plants, decreasing daylight hours and falling temperatures in the fall signal the initiation of the abscission process.Plant hormones, particularly abscisic acid (ABA) and ethylene, play a crucial role in regulating the abscission process. ABA promotes leaf abscission by signaling the abscission layer to form and weaken.Besides seasonal changes, leaf abscission can also be induced by various stress factors, including drought, herbivore damage, or disease. In these cases, abscission helps the plant shed damaged or unproductive leaves to redirect resources to healthier parts.Leaf abscission is a well-coordinated and vital process for the survival and growth of many plant species, as it allows them to adapt to changing seasons and environmental challenges. It is a fascinating aspect of plant biology and an essential part of the life cycle of deciduous and some evergreen plants.

questions.explanation: (c) : Senescence is the process of ageing which is caused by cellular breakdown, increased metabolic failure, increased entropy etc. It occurs in the period between reproductive maturity and death. Cell division followed by cell enlargement and differentiation, precede the actual separation. Senescence of cell in distal region lead to lignification of cell wall. Tylose formation in tracheary element and callose deposition in sieve elements which occur in advance of abscission (i.e. senescence) finally, lead to actual separation. Thus, vessels and tracheid (trachery elements) differentiation indicates senescence.

questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: "Mutated" is the past participle form of the verb "mutate." It refers to the process of undergoing a mutation, which is a permanent change in the genetic material (DNA) of an organism. Mutations can occur naturally due to errors in DNA replication, exposure to environmental factors (such as radiation or chemicals), or through genetic recombination.

The term "embolized" typically refers to the process of embolization, a medical procedure used to block or restrict blood flow to a specific area of the body. During embolization, a substance known as an embolic agent is introduced into a blood vessel to occlude or block it. The term "embolized" is used to describe the state of a blood vessel or specific area of the body after undergoing embolization. It means that the blood flow to that area has been intentionally obstructed or reduced for medical reasons.

"Etiolated" is a term used in botany to describe a condition in plants that have grown in darkness or low light conditions. When a plant is etiolated, it exhibits specific characteristics resulting from the lack of light.Etiolated plants often have a pale or yellowish coloration. This is because they produce less chlorophyll, the pigment responsible for the green color in leaves. Chlorophyll is essential for photosynthesis, and its reduced production results in the paleness of etiolated plants.Etiolation is a survival strategy that allows plants to conserve energy and prioritize elongating toward a light source when they are in low light conditions. However, it is not a healthy state for plants in the long term, as they require adequate light for photosynthesis and overall growth. Etiolated plants should be exposed to proper lighting to encourage normal development and prevent further etiolation.

"Defoliated" is a term used to describe the process or state of a plant losing its leaves. This can occur naturally, such as in deciduous trees shedding their leaves in the autumn.Defoliation can have various effects on plants, depending on the extent and cause. In some cases, it is a normal and temporary part of the plant's life cycle, while in others, it can be a response to stress or damage. It is important to understand the specific cause of defoliation and its impact on plant health in order to take appropriate measures for management or recovery.

questions.text: Tobacco plants resistant to a nematode have been developed by the introduction of DNA that produces (in the host cells):
questions.subject: Biology

Tobacco plants resistant to nematodes are not typically developed by introducing DNA that produces a specific hormone. The resistance is often achieved through other mechanisms.

An antifeedant is a substance that deters feeding by herbivores but is not a common approach for developing nematode-resistant tobacco plants. The primary strategy is introducing toxic proteins that harm the nematodes rather than deterring their feeding.
Nematode-resistant tobacco plants are often developed by introducing DNA that produces a toxic protein. This protein can be harmful to nematodes, effectively protecting the plant from their damage.

questions.explanation: Many nematodes live in plants and animals including human beings. A nematode Meloidogyne incognita infests the roots of tobacco plants and causes a great reduction in yield. A novel strategy was adopted to prevent this infection that was based on the process of RNA interference (RNAi). RNA interference (RNAi) is the phenomenon of inhibiting the activity of a gene by the synthesis of RNA molecules complementary to the mRNA. The normal (in vivo synthesized) mRNA of a gene is said to be “sense” because it carries the codons that are “read” during translation. Normally, the complement to the mRNA “sense” strand will not contain a sequence of codons that can be translated to produce a functional protein; thus, this complementary strand is called “anti­-sense RNA”. The anti-­sense RNA and mRNA molecules will anneal to form duplex RNA molecules (or double-stranded RNA) and the duplex RNA molecules can not be translated. Thus, the presence of anti­sense RNA will block translation of the mRNA of the affected gene. In fact, recent evidence indicates that these RNA duplexes are often rapidly degraded in vivo.

questions.text: If the diameter of the earth becomes two times its present value and its mass remains unchanged, then how would the weight of an object on the surface of the earth be affected?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If the diameter of the Earth becomes two times its present value, the distance from the center of the Earth to its surface would also double. questions.text: In photo-emission from a metal, if light of λ is replaced by light of wavelength λ / 4 , the maximum kinetic energy of the photoelectrons.
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Decreases by an amount equal to half of an incident photon of wavelength λ
options.explanation: When the wavelength of the incident light is decreased to λ/4 the energy of each photon increases. The energy of a photon is directly proportional to its frequency and inversely proportional to its wavelength. So, if the wavelength decreases to λ/4 the energy of each photon increases by a factor of 4. When a photoelectron is emitted, it gains energy from absorbing a photon. Since each photon now carries four times the energy compared to before, the photoelectron would gain four times the energy compared to when the wavelength was λ Thus, the correct choice would involve an increase in kinetic energy, not a decrease.

options.text: Increases by an amount equal to four times energy of an incident photon of wavelength λ
options.explanation: The maximum kinetic energy KEe of ejected electrons (photoelectrons) is given by KEe=hf-BE, where hf is the photon energy and BE is the binding energy (or work function) of the electron to the particular material. Since f = v/ X when X is replaced by λ/4, f increases by 4 times and hence the energy of incident photons increases by 4 times.

options.text: Increases by an amount equal to the work function of the metal
options.explanation: The work function represents the minimum energy required to eject an electron from the metal surface. However, changing the wavelength of the incident light doesn't change the work function of the metal. Therefore, this option doesn't accurately describe the effect of changing the wavelength of the incident light.

options.text: Decreases by an amount equal to the energy of an incident photon of wavelength λ
options.explanation: This choice contradicts the fact that decreasing the wavelength of the incident light increases the energy of each photon, which in turn increases the kinetic energy of the emitted photoelectrons. So, this option is not correct.

questions.text: Burning of which one of the following waste is considered as useful industrial fuel or to produce electricity:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Metals: Burning metals as a waste is not considered a useful industrial fuel or a common method to produce electricity. While some metals can be used as fuel sources in specific industrial processes (e.g., in metal smelting), it is not a widespread practice.

Grass: Burning grass, specifically in the form of biomass or agricultural waste, can be used as a renewable and sustainable energy source. It is commonly utilized in biomass power plants or for heating purposes. Grass and other plant materials contain organic matter that can be converted into biofuels or used directly as a fuel source.

Paper: Burning paper waste can also be used as a fuel source in certain contexts. Paper can be transformed into a form of biomass fuel, such as pellets or briquettes, which can be burned to generate heat or electricity. However, the recycling and reuse of paper waste are generally preferred over burning it for energy.

Plastic: Burning plastic waste is not considered a desirable or environmentally friendly method for fuel or electricity production. Burning plastic releases toxic gases and pollutants into the air, contributing to air pollution and posing risks to human health. Recycling and proper disposal of plastic waste are typically preferred over burning it.

questions.text: Starch gives a blue-black coloured compound on reacting with _ in aqueous solutions.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Starch gives a blue-black colored compound on reacting with iodine in aqueous solutions. This reaction is used to detect the presence of starch in a solution. The iodine molecule slips inside the amylose coil of the starch, forming a linear triiodide ion complex that slips into the coil of the starch causing an intense blue-black color. The blue-black color is due to the formation of an intermolecular charge-transfer complex between the iodine and starch molecules. When heated, the blue-black color disappears due to the breakdown of the starch-iodine complex. The iodine molecules are released from the amylose coil and form a brown-colored complex with iodide ions.

questions.text: Order of reactivity of halogens towards alkanes is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Alkanes undergo free radical substitution reactions with halogens, as they are sp3 hybridised and halogens can only be added in the molecule by replacing another group (substituting). The mechanism of free radical substitution involves 3 steps: initiation, propagation and termination. In the first step, the diatomic halogen molecules are split into two free radical species which can then add to the alkane molecules in the propagation steps. Down the group, the electronegativity decreases while the stability of these free radicals increases. The fluorine free radical is the least stable and most electronegative so it attacks the alkane more readily, and releases the highest amount of energy in the propagation step. The next is chlorine, followed by bromine and finally iodine, which is the least reactive. In Fact it is so unreactive that it is unable to extract a H atom from alkane. This order is consistent with option C, hence that is the correct answer.

questions.explanation: The order of reactivity of halogens towards alkanes, from highest to lowest, is as follows:1. Fluorine (F)2. Chlorine (Cl)3. Bromine (Br)4. Iodine (I)This order is based on the relative strengths of the carbon-halogen bonds in alkyl halides formed during the halogenation reaction. Fluorine is the most reactive halogen and is highly electronegative, leading to the formation of the strongest carbon-fluorine bond. Chlorine is less reactive than fluorine but still more reactive than bromine and iodine. Bromine is less reactive than both fluorine and chlorine but more reactive than iodine. Iodine is the least reactive halogen and tends to exhibit sluggish reactions with alkanes.

questions.text: Read the passage and answer the following question:“What a silly boy you are!” cried the miller. “I really don’t know what is the use of sending you to school. You seem not to learn anything. If little Hans came up here and saw our warm fire, our good supper, and our great cask of red wine, he might get envious. Envy is the most terrible thing and would spoil anybody’s nature. I certainly will not allow Hans’s nature to be spoiled. I am his best friend, and I will always watch over him, and see that he is not led into any temptations. Besides, if Hans came here, he might ask me to let him have some flour on credit, and that I could not do. Flour is one thing and friendship is another and they should not be confused. Why? The words are spelled differently and mean quite different things. Everybody can see that.” What does the statement “ Flour is one thing, and friendship is another” tell about the nature of Miller?

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the miller's actions and statements do not demonstrate kindness. Instead, he belittles the young boy and questions the value of sending him to school. He also expresses a self-centered attitude by prioritizing his own possessions over helping a friend in need. His dismissive and condescending tone when talking about little Hans suggests that he is not very kind.
The miller's words and behavior indicate a selfish nature, as he values his possessions and self-interest over being a true friend to little Hans.

This option is incorrect because the miller's actions and words do not align with the characteristics of a friendly person. While he claims to be little Hans's best friend, his behavior shows a lack of genuine concern for the boy. He is more interested in boasting about his possessions and protecting them from Hans's potential envy than being a true friend who supports and cares for his friend's well-being.

This option is incorrect because the miller's actions and attitude do not exhibit a caring nature. He does not show empathy towards little Hans, and his primary focus is on safeguarding his own interests rather than looking out for Hans's best interests. The miller's refusal to help little Hans with flour on credit and his insensitivity towards Hans's potential feelings of envy suggest that he is not a caring individual.

questions.explanation: If the miller was kind, he would have lent the flour. If the miller was friendly and caring, as he claims in the previous lines by saying “I will not allow Hans’s nature to be spoiled”, he would certainly let Hans have some flour on credit if he asked him for it. Since he is saying that he will not give flour on credit even to someone he cares for, this makes him selfish.

options.text: Because Miller thinks that after seeing the luxuries of his home Hans will get envious

questions.text: UDOMETER is coded as DUMOTERE then how will SUBLEASE be coded?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: The correct answer is (d) USLBEAES. The coding pattern used here is that each group of three letters is reversed and then the first and last letters of the resulting group are interchanged. Applying this pattern to "UDOMETER", we get "DUMOTERE" as given. Applying the same pattern to "SUBLEASE", we get: "SUB" reversed becomes "BUS" "LEA" reversed becomes "AEL" "SE" reversed becomes "ES" So the coded word is "BUSAELES". Interchanging the first and last letters of each group, we get "USLBEAES". Therefore, the correct answer is (d).

questions.explanation: The way this is coded is that alternate letters in the word are moved one word forward. So U went after D, O went after M, E went after T, and the second last E goes after R. Hence for SUBLEASE, S will go after U, B will go after L, E will go after A, and S will go after E, giving us USLBAEES.
